item.description:
## DuoUpdater 0.1.9
**Apps that update themselves now clear correctly.** If an app updated through its own updater (for example, Chrome via "About Chrome") while DuoUpdater was busy installing other updates, it could keep showing a stale "update available" row long after it was already current. DuoUpdater now re-checks the moment the installs finish, so the row clears right away instead of lingering.
**"Update All" shows the whole queue.** Every app in an "Update All" run now shows a "Queued" state immediately, instead of leaving the ones further down the list looking idle with a clickable Update button. Clicking Update on an app that's already queued can no longer start a second install of it.

## DuoUpdater 0.1.8
**Fixes a hang (app not responding).** Opening the menu or window while a background update check was in flight could, in a timing-dependent race, send the refresh logic into an infinite loop and freeze the app (requiring a force-quit). The single-flight refresh now clears its in-flight marker correctly, so the coalesced follow-up can never recurse. This bug was latent since 0.1.4; if you ever saw DuoUpdater stop responding, this is why.

## DuoUpdater 0.1.7
**iPhone/iPad apps on Mac now redirect to the App Store.** Wrapped iOS apps running on Apple Silicon (e.g. Aqara Home) can only be updated by the App Store app itself — the `mas` command-line tool has no Mac-store entry for them and fails with "No apps found for ADAM ID …". These apps no longer show a one-click Update that errors out; instead the row offers an **App Store** button that opens the app's page, where the real Update lives. They're also excluded from Install-All so a batch can't stall on them.
